concept_candidates.label: Nova
concept_candidates.description: An astronomical event in which a white dwarf in a close binary system accretes matter from its companion, triggering a sudden brightening of a new star that fades over weeks or months. It is a type of cataclysmic variable star.
concept_candidates.justification: Nova is a stable, well‑defined astronomical event that recurs across many documents and can be represented as a reusable class in the ontology.

ontology_relation_candidates.label: is a
ontology_relation_candidates.description: Relates an individual entity to a class or subclass, indicating that the entity is an instance of that class or belongs to that subclass.
ontology_relation_candidates.justification: The relation "is a" is a widely used, semantically stable subsumptive relation that can be applied across many documents to express class membership or subclass relationships. It is reusable and meaningful beyond isolated mentions, aligning with standard ontology practices for instance-of or subclass-of relations.

ontology_relation_candidates.label: locatedIn
ontology_relation_candidates.description: Relates an entity (person, organization, place, or other entity) to the geographic or administrative location where it is situated, headquartered, or based.
ontology_relation_candidates.justification: The relation is a common, semantically stable spatial containment that appears across many documents. It aligns with existing Wikidata properties such as P131 (located in the administrative territorial entity) and P276 (location), and is distinct from generic location labels. The candidate’s aliases and synonyms reinforce its reuse and clarity.

ontology_relation_candidates.justification: The relation is semantically stable, widely reusable across documents, and aligns with existing ontology properties such as Wikidata P361. It captures a meaningful component or membership relationship beyond a single mention.
